connectorUiConfig.title: Cloudflare (Using Blob Container) (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The Cloudflare data connector provides the capability to ingest Cloudflare logs into Microsoft Sentinel using the Cloudflare Logpush and Azure Blob Storage. Refer to [Cloudflare documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/about/)for more information.

NOTE: The Cloudflare (Using Blob Container) (via Codeless Connector Framework) data connector available in the solution requires the Azure Blob Storage account and the Microsoft Sentinel workspace to be in the same Azure subscription and the same Resource Group. Deploying across different subscriptions or resource groups may result in errors such as CreateDataFlowResources not defined during connector configuration.

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Create a storage account and a container
customs.description: Before setting up logpush in Cloudflare, first create a storage account and a container in Microsoft Azure. Use [this guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/storage-blobs-introduction) to know more about Container and Blob. Follow the steps in the [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-create?tabs=azure-portal) to create an Azure Storage account.

customs.name: Generate a Blob SAS URL
customs.description: Create and Write permissions are required. Refer the [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/translator/document-translation/how-to-guides/create-sas-tokens?tabs=Containers) to know more about Blob SAS token and url.

properties.description: @{workbookKey=CloudflareCCFWorkbook; logoFileName=cloudflare.svg; description=Gain insights into Cloudflare events using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF).; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=1.1.0; title=Cloudflare CCF; templateRelativePath=CloudflareCCF.json; subtitle=; provider=Cloudflare}.description
Query searches for client related errors.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where HttpStatusCode between(400..499)
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr
| extend UrlCustomEntity = ClientRequestURI

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches for client related errors.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 1
Query searches for client TLS errors.

properties.query:
let err_tls = dynamic(['UNKNOWN', 'INTERNAL_ERROR', 'INVALID_CONFIG', 'INVALID_SNI', 'HANDSHAKE_FAILED']);
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where ClientTlsStatus in~ (err_tls)
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr
| extend UrlCustomEntity = ClientRequestURI

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches for client TLS errors.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 2
Query searches for files requested.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| extend File = extract(@'\/(\w+\.\w+)', 1, ClientRequestURI)
| summarize count() by File, SrcIpAddr
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr
| extend FileCustomEntity = File

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 3
Query searches rare user agent strings.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(HttpUserAgentOriginal)
| summarize count() by HttpUserAgentOriginal, SrcIpAddr
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches rare user agent strings.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 4
Query searches for server related errors.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where HttpStatusCode between(500..599)
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr
| extend UrlCustomEntity = ClientRequestURI

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches for server related errors.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 5
Query searches for server TLS errors.

properties.query:
let err_tls = dynamic(['UNKNOWN', 'INTERNAL_ERROR', 'INVALID_CONFIG', 'INVALID_SNI', 'HANDSHAKE_FAILED']);
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where OriginTlsStatus in~ (err_tls)
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr
| extend UrlCustomEntity = ClientRequestURI

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches for server TLS errors.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 6
Query searches top network rules triggered.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(NetworkRuleName)
| summarize count() by NetworkRuleName, EdgeRequestHost
| order by count_
| extend CloudAppCustomEntity = EdgeRequestHost

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches top network rules triggered.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 7
Query searches top WAF rules triggered.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(WAFRuleID)
| summarize count() by WAFRuleID, WAFRuleMessage, EdgeRequestHost
| order by count_
| extend CloudAppCustomEntity = EdgeRequestHost

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches top WAF rules triggered.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 8
Query searches requests by country and helps to identify requests coming from unexpected countries.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize count() by SrcGeoCountry, SrcIpAddr
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches requests by country and helps to identify requests coming from unexpected countries.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 9
Query searches for unexpected EdgeResponseStatus values.

properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where HttpStatusCode != EdgeResponseStatus
| extend IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr
| extend UrlCustomEntity = ClientRequestURI

tags.name: description
tags.value: Query searches for unexpected EdgeResponseStatus values.

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Hunting Query 10
Detects requests from IP with bad reputation index.
Detects requests from IP with bad reputation index.
properties.query:
let ip_reputation = dynamic(['unknown', 'badHost', 'greylist', 'securityScanner', 'scan', 'tor']);
Cloudflare
| where ClientIPClass in~ (ip_reputation)
| extend CompleteUrl = strcat(HttpRequestHeaderHost,ClientRequestPath)

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 1
Detects requests where user agent is empty.
Detects requests where user agent is empty.
properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where isempty(HttpUserAgentOriginal)

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 2
Detects multiple failure requests from single source in short timeframe.
Detects multiple failure requests from single source in short timeframe.
properties.displayName: Cloudflare - Multiple error requests from single source
properties.query:
let threshold = 100;
Cloudflare
| where HttpRequestMethod =~ 'GET'
| summarize err_cnt = count() by SrcIpAddr, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where err_cnt > threshold

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 3

properties.displayName: Cloudflare - Multiple error requests from single source

properties.description: Detects requests with different user agents from one source in short timeframe.
Detects requests with different user agents from one source in short timeframe.
properties.displayName: Cloudflare - Multiple user agents for single source
properties.query:
let threshold = 10;
Cloudflare
| where isnotempty(HttpUserAgentOriginal)
| summarize d_ua = dcount(HttpUserAgentOriginal) by SrcIpAddr, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where d_ua > threshold

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 4

properties.displayName: Cloudflare - Multiple user agents for single source

properties.description: Detects requests from countries which are in blocklist.
Detects requests from countries which are in blocklist.
properties.displayName: Cloudflare - Client request from country in blocklist
properties.query:
let bl_countries = dynamic(['cn', 'hk']);
Cloudflare
| where SrcGeoCountry in~ (bl_countries)
| extend CompleteUrl = strcat(HttpRequestHeaderHost,ClientRequestPath)

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 5

properties.displayName: Cloudflare - Client request from country in blocklist

properties.description: Detects post requests to unusual extensions.
Detects post requests to unusual extensions.
properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where HttpRequestMethod in~ ('POST', 'PUT')
| where tostring(HttpStatusCode) startswith '2'
| where DstBytes != 0 or SrcBytes != 0
| extend fe = extract(@'.*(\.\w+)$', 1, ClientRequestURI)
| where fe in~ ('.jpg', '.jpeg', '.gif', '.png', '.icon', '.ico', '.xml', '.swf', '.svg', '.ppt', '.pttx', '.doc', '.docx', '.rtf', '.pdf', '.tif', '.zip', '.mov')

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 6
Detects client requests to unusual client request.
Detects client requests to unusual client request.
properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where HttpRequestMethod =~ 'GET'
| where DstBytes != 0 or SrcBytes != 0
| where ClientRequestURI has_any ('/admin', '/admin.php', 'wp-admin', '.htaccess', '/etc/shadow', '/etc/passwd', '/etc/hosts', '/etc/ssh/')

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 7
Detects client requests to unusual URI.
Detects client requests to unusual URI.
Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 8
Detects WAF "Allowed" action on threat events.
Detects WAF "Allowed" action on threat events.
properties.query:
Cloudflare
| where isnotempty(WAFRuleID) or isnotempty(WAFRuleMessage)
| where WAFAction =~ 'Allow'
| extend CompleteUrl = strcat(HttpRequestHeaderHost,ClientRequestPath)

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 9
Detects XSS probing patterns.
Detects XSS probing patterns.
properties.displayName: Cloudflare - XSS probing pattern in request
properties.query:
let s_threshold = 3;
Cloudflare
| where HttpRequestMethod in~ ('POST', 'PUT')
| extend susp_ch = countof(ClientRequestURI, '%00')
| where ClientRequestURI matches regex @'(alert\()|(alert\%28)|(String\.fromCharCode\()|(expression\(alert)' or susp_ch > s_threshold 
| extend CompleteUrl = strcat(HttpRequestHeaderHost,ClientRequestPath)

properties.description: Cloudflare CCF Analytics Rule 10

properties.displayName: Cloudflare - XSS probing pattern in request

properties.descriptionHtml:
Cloudflare, Inc. (NYSE: NET) is the leading connectivity cloud company on a mission to help build a better Internet. It empowers organizations to make their employees, applications and networks faster and more secure everywhere, while reducing complexity and cost. Cloudflare’s connectivity cloud delivers the most full-featured, unified platform of cloud-native products and developer tools, so any organization can gain the control they need to work, develop, and accelerate their business.

Powered by one of the world’s largest and most interconnected networks, Cloudflare blocks billions of threats online for its customers every day. It is trusted by millions of organizations – from the largest brands to entrepreneurs and small businesses to nonprofits, humanitarian groups, and governments across the globe.

Learn more about Cloudflare’s connectivity cloud at cloudflare.com/connectivity-cloud.

Our new Sentinel integration with Microsoft's CCF platform enables advanced security analytics & incident response capabilities for Cloudflare protected properties.

This connector provides benefits such as easier implementation and maintenance, configurable UI components, and a simpler experience for customers.

See release notes here
